body: Applies verified A-tier HSD PObj names and updates active source references so objdiff progress remains unchanged.

Recovers the previous 3-month decompilation campaign into the reset dtk-template repo, banked **properly through the DTK pipeline** (not hand-linked). Matched functions **110 → 3,306 (1.28% → 38.43%)**; matched code 0.14% → 9.98%.

## How
- Every recovered function is `Object(CodeCandidate, ...)` — compared by objdiff but **not linked** — so the retail `main.dol` sha1 gate is untouched (`ninja` → `main.dol: OK`).
- Restores the correct **per-library MWCC compiler version** via per-object `mw_version=` overrides (the reset had defaulted them wrong). From the archive's `compile_config.json`:

| Library | Compiler |
|---|---|
| game/*, trk, hsd, crt/stdio | GC/1.3 |
| crt/mem | GC/2.0 |
| crt/exit | GC/1.3.2 |
| dolphin/* | GC/1.2.5n |

- New split units carve genuinely-decompiled TUs out of auto-generated units; `object_map.freeze.json` is regenerated and **topology-delta-verified** (`total_functions = 8603` invariant preserved).
- Every counted function is byte-exact (`fuzzy_match_percent == 100`), build-gated. `symbols.txt` is untouched.

## Integrity note
An initial pass mistakenly counted 35 `asm { #include "*.inc" }` wrappers in `gs_field_world.c` as matches (asm≡asm = 100%). **CI caught them** (the `.inc` are gitignored, so a clean checkout can't build). Commit `cb421e03` removed those 63 undecompiled asm functions (to forward declarations) and fixed a Shift-JIS encoding error. The **3,306 counted functions are all genuine byte-exact C** — verified by building with **all `.inc` removed**.

## Verification
- clean-checkout build (all `.inc` absent) compiles with zero errors
- `ninja` → `main.dol: OK`
- `check_object_map_freeze.py` → OK (318 units, 8603 functions)
- report.json: 3,306 functions at fuzzy 100% (independent recount confirms)

## Scope / notes
- ~38% of *functions* but ~10% of *code* — small/leaf functions recovered; the large hard functions remain (fleet/permuter work).
- Near-misses (97–99%) are code-correctness gaps, not compiler-flag issues.
- Deferred (draft-only or asm-heavy): `sound_se`, `sound.c` core, `people.c`, `colosseum_script.c`, `menu_bag.c`.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01BmK65CSwEuMcaXE1BsKWgm

## Summary

- Preserve the six validated byte-exact previous-campaign wins in `archive/previous_campaign`.
- Keep this as archive/research state only; it does not alter active DTK-template source units or progress numbers.

## Validation

- `git diff --check origin/master...origin/codex/validated-matching-wins-20260630`
- `.inc` staged/diff scan: none
- `git merge-tree` against current `origin/master`: clean archive-only merge

### Report for GC6E01 (272bcb0 - 4a48c71)

No changes

## Summary

- Reclaim eight exact DB/OS `CodeCandidate` units from the previous campaign.
- Keep the recovered units non-linked under the standard dtk-template reporting path.
- Leave `__OSSetExceptionHandler` out because its split range still makes `mwldeppc` spin during full link validation.

## Exact Units

- `dolphin/os/OSGetExceptionHandler.c` - `20/20`, `1/1`
- `dolphin/os/OSAlarmCreate.c` - `16/16`, `1/1`
- `dolphin/os/OSContextCurrent.c` - `12/12`, `1/1`
- `dolphin/os/OSContextClear.c` - `36/36`, `1/1`
- `dolphin/os/OSInterruptHandlers.c` - `48/48`, `2/2`
- `dolphin/os/OSState_fn_8009FAEC.c` - `12/12`, `1/1`
- `dolphin/os/OSThreadQueue.c` - `28/28`, `2/2`
- `dolphin/db/DBPrintf.c` - `80/80`, `1/1`

## Validation

- `python3 configure.py --no-progress`
- `ninja all_source build/GC6E01/report.json`
- `cp build/GC6E01/report.json report.json`
- `ninja` -> `build/GC6E01/main.dol: OK`
- `git diff --check`
- asm/inc scan on new source files

## Deferred

`__OSSetExceptionHandler` at `0x8009A0C4..0x8009A0E0` is report-exact in isolation attempts, but the split range shares its start address with the global label `__OSDBJUMPEND`. Adding that split causes the linker to spin, so it is intentionally excluded from this PR.

## Summary
- restore two template-compatible SDK source units from the previous campaign archive
- add dtk text splits for `dolphin/os/OSArena.c` and `trk/udp_cc.c`
- register both as `CodeCandidate` only, so they are compared and reported but not linked into `main.dol`
- regenerate root `report.json` from the dtk-template report path

## Verified Metrics
- `OSArena`: 4/4 functions, 32/32 code bytes, 100%
- `udp_cc`: 9/9 functions, 72/72 code bytes, 100%
- overall matched functions: 9 -> 22
- overall matched code bytes: 616 -> 720
- total units: 87 -> 90

## Validation
- `python3 configure.py --no-progress`
- `ninja all_source build/GC6E01/report.json`
- `ninja` (`build/GC6E01/main.dol: OK`)
- `python3 configure.py progress`
- active scan for `.inc` includes / inline asm in `src include configure.py tools`
- `git diff --check`

## Notes
These units were trialed as linked `Matching` substitutions and the final DOL SHA failed, so this PR intentionally keeps them as non-linked candidates. That preserves the canonical build while making the honest report/decomp.dev progress visible.

## Summary
- archive the previous custom campaign tooling/docs/PC-port experiment under `archive/previous_campaign/`
- restore the live project shape to the dtk-template-style pipeline (`configure.py`, `ninja`, generated `report.json`)
- keep recovered symbol names in the active `symbols.txt` while removing old split/report/config variants from the live tree
- refresh README, CONTRIBUTING, AGENTS, `.gitignore`, and root `report.json` around the reset baseline

## Validation
- `python3 configure.py --no-progress`
- `ninja all_source build/GC6E01/report.json`
- `ninja`
- `python3 configure.py progress`
- `git diff --check`
- active scan for `.inc` includes / inline asm in `src include configure.py tools`
- GitHub Actions: `build (GC6E01)` passed

## Summary
- add top-level data measures to report.json so decomp.dev can render the Code/Data bars
- sync README and tools/decomp_work/progress.json with the data denominator
- harden report generation against stale objdiff symbol parsing and accidental denominator shrink from partial local base objects

## Report numbers
- Function match: 4,308 / 6,795 (63.4%)
- Code match: 542,148 / 1,368,604 bytes (39.6%)
- Data match: 0 / 1,276,195 bytes (0.0%)

## Validation
- python3 -m py_compile tools/gen_decomp_report.py tools/check_report_sanity.py tools/sync_progress_metadata.py tools/measure_cache.py
- python3 tools/check_report_sanity.py report.json
- python3 tools/sync_progress_metadata.py --check
- python3 tools/gen_decomp_report.py -o report.json (expected refusal on current partial local base: prevents 4,875-function denominator overwrite)

## Summary
- Adds `tools/sync_progress_metadata.py` as the canonical sync/check command for README + `tools/decomp_work/progress.json` from `report.json`.
- Updates `progress.json` from 11 stale completed entries to 3,432 active real-C completed functions derived from current `report.json`; asm wrappers and stubs are explicitly excluded from completed.
- Makes `tools/check_report_sanity.py` also fail when README/progress metadata is stale, so the existing report-sanity workflow enforces sync without changing workflow files.
- Wires auto update paths (`surgical_report_update.py`, `update_report.py`, `auto_push.sh`, `report_cadence.py`) through the sync command.

## Validation
- `python3 tools/sync_progress_metadata.py --check`
- `python3 tools/check_report_sanity.py report.json`
- `python3 -m py_compile tools/check_report_sanity.py tools/sync_progress_metadata.py tools/decomp_work/surgical_report_update.py tools/decomp_work/update_report.py tools/decomp_work/report_cadence.py`

- Adds no `.inc` files, asm wrappers, inline asm, or new raw offset-pointer additions.

## Summary

This branch banks the current low-bucket decomp fleet wins and supporting fleet/dashboard tooling.

## What
Wires the cockpit's **GLM** (z.ai coding plan) into the matching-decomp pipeline, and makes the per-function compile/measure gate run on macOS/Linux.

### `tools/lane_glm.py` (new)
Same hardened **"remote generation, LOCAL gated verification"** loop the retired Ollama lanes used, with GLM as the generator:
```
target PPC asm + current C  --HTTPS-->  z.ai GLM (default glm-5.2)
                            <--cand C--
replace fn body -> compile (wibo) -> objdiff measure ->
KEEP iff fn strictly improves AND file matched-count doesn't regress, else revert
```
GLM only proposes; the local gate decides — a bad candidate can never damage the tree (every non-improving trial is reverted). Key read from `~/.config/decomp-keys/cockpit.env`. Targets a match-% band (`--min-pct/--max-pct`, `--best-first`) or a single `--symbol`; `--apply` keeps wins.

### `tools/compile_check.py` (Mac-native gate)
- Resolve `mwcceppc.exe` from `build/compilers/GC/<ver>/` (configure.py download) in addition to `tools/mwcc_compiler/`.
- Run `mwcceppc.exe` (a Windows PE) through **wibo** on non-Windows.
- Resolve `objdiff-cli` from `build/tools/` as well as `tools/`.

## Verified (macOS arm64)
- `colosseum_battle.c` compiles via wibo → 648 functions, 316 fully matched.
- Full GLM-5.2 loop runs: generate → compile → objdiff → gate (correctly reverts a non-improving candidate).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

## Repro
On a clean checkout with the ROM in place, `ninja` fails:
```
[N/M] REPORT
FAILED: build/GC6E01/report.json
build/tools/objdiff-cli report generate -o build/GC6E01/report.json
Failed: Failed to open ./build/GC6E01/base/crt/__init_cpp_exceptions.o
  Caused by: No such file or directory (os error 2)
```
Reproduced on macOS arm64; same error reported on Windows 11 in #5.

## Root cause
- `configure.py` sets `config.libs = []` — the all-asm byte-match baseline, which compiles **no** source objects (`build/GC6E01/base/` stays empty).
- configure.py preserves a **curated `objdiff.json`** (63 units) whose `base_path` points at those source objects.
- The default ninja target is `progress` → `objdiff-cli report generate`, which opens each unit's base object and dies on the first missing one.
- `main.dol` itself builds and byte-matches (`build.sha1: OK`); only the report step fails. The maintainer measures progress via `tools/gen_decomp_report.py`, not the ninja report, so never hit it.

## Fix
Disable the ninja `progress`/`report` target when no matching libs are declared; the default target falls back to the `ok` sha check. Re-enables automatically once libs are populated. One guarded line in configure.py.

## Verification
Fresh `ninja` on macOS arm64 (wibo + CodeWarrior + dtk + objdiff):
```
[1/2] CHECK config/GC6E01/build.sha1
build/GC6E01/main.dol: OK
EXIT=0
```
`main.dol` sha = `870e8b9693ca780782d80f22a6a4572d8ba9458f` (matches the original).

Fixes #5

## What
A self-contained macOS/Linux cockpit for driving **GLM + Codex** on the decomp pipeline: `tools/decomp_work/tmux_control/cockpit.sh` (+ `COCKPIT_MAC.md`).

Replaces the Windows `control.sh`/`txk.sh`/psmux setup (whose worker scripts were never committed and which existed only to wrangle a native-PE tmux). Uses real `tmux` directly — no SIGKILL wrapper, no WinGet path hunting. Panes are tagged with a tmux `@role` option that TUIs can't overwrite.

## Commands
- **`ask-glm` / `ask-codex`** — reliable headless dispatch (`opencode run` / `codex exec`), prints the agent's answer.
- **`bootstrap` / `attach`** — interactive 2×2 TUI grid (orchestrator | glm | codex | shell).
- `send` / `capture` / `idle` / `status` / `restart` / `kill`.

## Wiring
- GLM via opencode → z.ai coding endpoint, default model `glm/glm-5.2`.
- Keys sourced from `~/.config/decomp-keys/cockpit.env` (outside the repo; never committed).
- Codex requires a one-time `codex login`.

Verified: `ask-glm` returns correct GLM-5.2 output; bootstrap/list/status/kill all work.

## Why
Moving primary dev from Windows to macOS, and making the repo cloneable/contributable on any OS. The core `ninja` build (`tools/project.py`) was already cross-platform via the `EXE`/`is_windows()` helper and the wibo wrapper — but ~48 **decomp-workflow** scripts hardcoded `.exe` suffixes and Windows paths, so they broke on macOS/Linux.

## What
- **Native tool resolution** (`objdiff-cli`, `dtk`): drop the `.exe` suffix except on Windows (`os.name == "nt"`). 42 tool scripts.
  - `mwcceppc`/`mwldeppc`/`sjiswrap` **intentionally keep `.exe`** — they are Windows PE binaries executed via **wibo** on mac/linux, so the filename must not change.
- **`scan_all_files.py`**: Windows backslash paths → `pathlib`.
- **`decomp.sh`**: default to PATH `python3`/`python`; `$WINPY` is now an override for the Windows-stub/WSL case rather than a hardcoded default.
- **Env-driven hosts**: replace hardcoded `10.0.0.3` with `DECOMP_GPU_HOST` / `DECOMP_OLLAMA_PORT` / `DECOMP_GPU_SSH` (default `192.168.50.101`), and `CODEX_CMD` (defaults to `codex` on non-Windows).

## Safety
- No functional change on Windows.
- Every modified `.py` passes `python -m py_compile`.
- 47 files changed, +108/-62.

## Related
Helps the setup blocker in #5 (cross-platform contributor onboarding). A separate follow-up will address the `report`/`progress` first-run build-ordering race itself.

Closes out this campaign's work: the orchestration pipeline is hardened, near-miss work is no longer discarded, the cost model is fixed, and a batch of real decomp wins landed. 62 commits, +6531/-1242 across 62 files.

## Decomp wins (25 commits)
- **colosseum_battle: +53 byte-exact** (the big Ghidra-draft reshape, gated).
- MObj wrappers converted to real C (init / removal / material-copy).
- Pokemon: stat/status/reset loop matches + reg-alloc frees; jump-table walls stopped from re-grinding.
- GSthread sibling stubs (gs_texture, gs_thread destroy/unlink) matched to 100%.
- gs_material single-apply relocation matched.
- Overall ~39% real-C match held through a farm regression that was diagnosed + reverted.

## Pipeline hardening (fleet / dashboard)
- **pane_io wedge-watchdog** — auto-restarts the capture loop when it wedges under load (root cause of "agents idle too long").
- **Dispatch correctness** — marks off the critical path (spawn-tax fix), drain only when a lane is *currently* idle (stop `/clear` wiping in-progress work), sonnet_queue fallback so lanes never starve, cooldown/interval tuning.
- **Reshape mode** — from-scratch m2c-draft → real-C for LOW Ghidra drafts, behind `.reshape_first`.
- **Dashboard** — re-bucket band_wins → DONE live (fixed a frozen, ~400-win-understated DONE count).

## Near-miss banking (stop discarding work)
- `band.py bank` (per-fn) + `bank-file` (whole-file reshape) capture 90-99% real-C the fleet can't close.
- `bank_nearmiss.py` splices them into canon with **strict no-regress** + asm/.inc fraud guard, feeding the permuter queue. Dry-run-gated.

## Tier-2: cost + correctness
- **#6 atomic file-based locks** — `locks.py` rewritten off sqlite to O_EXCL files; `list` leaves the dispatch hot path (validated 10/10).
- **#5 single fresh source of truth** — `measure_db.py` write-on-every-measure cache; dashboard reads it so buckets reflect reality between ledger rebuilds.
- **#4 batched cheap-agent cracking** — `lever-crack-batch` named workflow (Sonnet crack + Haiku independent verify) for the mechanical REG-COLORING subset; `ECONOMIC_MODEL.md` documents permuter (free) / batch (cheap) / Opus (reshape).

## Notes
- Some uncommitted WIP (partial fsys_file / DVDLow, scratch research docs) is intentionally left out of this PR.

## What

`progress.py` counted **every** `build/GC6E01/base/*.o` as a real translation unit (skip list was just `test_va2/3.o`). The band harness left **3 transient integration scratch objects** there: `band_trainer_integrated` (380 fns, dup of `game/trainer`), `manual_integrated` (380, dup of `game/trainer`), `band_mtool` (23). They were counted as phantom units → **+783 phantom functions** in the denominator (**7969 → 8752**) → published code% silently fell ~53% → ~44% on 2026-06-16.

## Not a regression — proof
- DOL builds **byte-for-byte identical to the ROM** (sha1 `870e8b96` = `config/GC6E01/build.sha1`).
- fuzzy% stayed flat (~84%); matched-function count actually rose. Pure measurement accounting, not lost work.

## Changes
- **`tools/progress.py`** — `iter_base_objects()` skips scratch objects via `SKIP_BASE_SCRATCH` (`band_*`, `*_integrated`, `_int_*`, `_evt_*`, `cs_*`). `gen_decomp_report.py` reuses this enumeration, so the generator is fixed too.
- **`report.json`** — drop the 3 phantom units; recompute aggregates → **total_functions 7969**, matched 4952, fn% 62.14, code% 44.56, fuzzy% 84.51.
- **`tools/check_report_sanity.py` + `.github/workflows/report-sanity.yml`** — new pure-Python CI gate failing on any scratch-pattern unit, out-of-sync denominator, or duplicate unit signature. Regression-tested both ways.

## Note
This fixes the **function denominator**. The byte-weighted code% (53→44) is a separate W-SDA-WRAPPER measurement-wall artifact on ~15 PC-port TUs (also not lost work — DOL byte-matches), tracked separately.

## What changed
- Made `fn_80017990` byte-match by preserving GLM-5.2's compiler-sensitive compare-load spelling.
- Added a `SummaryPageEntry` view for `lbl_80266918` and routed the matched gauge helpers through named accessors so the function bodies no longer expose literal `0x4C` byte arithmetic.
- Added the GLM-5.2 candidate and benchmark note under `tools/decomp_work/kg/candidates/fn_80017990/`.

## Acceptance status
This PR is a byte-match evidence PR, not a final PC-port/readability acceptance PR.

The current source still contains transitional accessor internals that depend on MWCC-sensitive address spelling. That is useful as an oracle for type recovery, but it should remain marked as portability debt until `lbl_80266918` can be represented by a real typed data definition without unresolved pointer arithmetic and without losing behavior.

## Why
GLM-5.2 isolated a single `add` operand-order mismatch from the diff-aware repair loop. The pointer-plus spelling makes CodeWarrior emit base-first while preserving the load displacement. A follow-up readability pass proved direct typed-array indexing changes MWCC register allocation, so the accepted source centralizes the sensitive byte addressing behind named table accessors instead.

## Validation
- `python tools\compile_check.py src\game\gs_pokemon_summary.c --diff --symbols fn_80015050 fn_80016618 fn_8001793C fn_80017990`: `fn_8001793C` and `fn_80017990` remain 100%; neighboring converted functions stayed at baseline.
- `python tools\compile_check.py src\game\gs_pokemon_summary.c --diff --require-match --symbols fn_8001793C fn_80017990`: both required matches passed.
- `python tools\decomp_work\overnight\verify_match.py gs_pokemon_summary fn_80017990`: 100.0%.
- `git fsck --connectivity-only --no-dangling`

## Notes
- Rejected direct `SummaryPageEntry` array indexing because it regressed `fn_80017990` to 80.6452% by changing register allocation and `lbzx` shape.
- Rejected a volatile typed table view because it regressed `fn_80017990` to 73.5294% and added instructions.
- The regression means more type/data recovery work remains; it should not be dismissed just because objdiff reaches 100%.

## Migrate to the canonical dtk-template + copyright cleanup

Makes the project a proper [encounter/dtk-template](https://github.com/encounter/dtk-template) decomp, and removes committed game-derived binary/asm from the (public) repo.

### Build — now canonical
`python configure.py && ninja` drives decomp-toolkit: split the DOL → patch ldscript → link → `elf2dol` → verify SHA-1. Reproduces `build/GC6E01/main.dol` **byte-exact** (`870e8b96…`).
- `tools/project.py` + helpers brought in verbatim from the template; `configure.py` rewritten to the canonical `ProjectConfig`/`generate_build` form.
- dtk, objdiff-cli and the Metrowerks compilers **auto-download** per-platform (`dtk_tag`/`objdiff_tag`/`compilers_tag`) — none are vendored on the repo.
- One tiny, documented divergence from upstream `project.py`: `tools/split_dol.py` wraps the split to patch the ldscript debug-stack gap `0x2000 → 0x8000` (the retail value; dtk has no knob).
- `--no-update` so builds don't churn tracked config.

### Copyright
- **Removed** the verbatim extracted game binary: raw DOL `.bin` segments + the Ghidra project image.
- **Untracked** the 7,508 `.inc` + 23 `asm/*.s` per-function disassembly — kept local (gitignored), off the repo. *(git history of these paths is scrubbed separately — master-only.)*

### Cleanup
- PC port moved `src/pcport` → `pcport/` (its own concern; separate build). `src/` now holds only the game's own C.
- Pruned 45 obsolete one-time migration scripts.
- README aligned; CI (`build.yml`) → config-validation (the byte-match `ninja` needs your ROM, which isn't on the public repo).

### Honest findings (why this isn't a 100%-tiled decomp)
- The retail linker **interleaved some TUs** (`OSException.c`'s code sits inside `OSInit.c`'s `.text` range), so dtk can't carve clean per-file objects — per-file splits / matched-C linking are infeasible without large de-interleaving work. The build stays all-asm (and byte-matches); `config.libs` is empty.
- The committed `.inc` are a project-specific mwasm function-body format (inline `asm{}` includes), incompatible with dtk's GNU-as asm — regenerating them from the ROM would need a custom PPC→mwasm disassembler (deferred).

The legacy `tools/decomp_work/build_dol.sh` is untouched and still byte-matches.

thx @encounter, @cuyler on the gc/wii decomp discord for pointing me in the right direction!
